text: The surgeon circumferentially dissects the main renal vessels to facilitate the placement of bulldog clamps. Girota's fascia is opened away from the tumor site, and dissection proceeds along the renal capsule until the mass is exposed. Fat is cleared around the mass, revealing one to two centimeters of normal renal parenchyma for future reconstruction.

The surgeon scores the margins of the Reynolds capsule with electrocautery to delineate the resection boundaries. Two laparoscopic bulldog clamps are applied for Hiller clamping. Tumors are resected along the scored margins using cold scissors, while the bedside assistant uses suction to clear the resection bed and applies slight counter-retraction for improved visualization.

The surgeon performs renography in two layers using robotic needle drivers. A 20 cm, 2-0 valve suture on an SH-I needle is used as a running suture to secure the tumor excision bed, overseeing larger vessels and entries into the collecting system. The renal capsule is re-approximated with a continuous horizontal matrix suture using a CT-1 needle, with sliding hemlock clips applied after each suture is passed through the capsule.

Following the completion of renography, the surgeon unclamps the hilum and inspects the renal excision bed for hemostasis. The defect is then covered with fluocele and surgicel to ensure proper sealing and hemostatic control.
